forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,67 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: d180
+title: 'Inspector layer fails to contain focus: `s`-jump marks the base layer, Escape leaks, arrow-nav escapes'
+---
+## What
+
+The inspector overlay is supposed to act as a modal focus layer (`name="inspector"` mounted in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.tsx:250`), but in practice focus is leaking out to the parent `window` layer while at least one inspector panel is open. Three observable symptoms confirm this:
+
+1. **Jump-To paints pills on the base layer.** Pressing `s` (vim) or `Mod+G` (cua/emacs) opens `<JumpToOverlay>`, which enumerates scopes via `useJumpTargets` in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.tsx:524-566`. That hook takes `priorFocusedFq` and resolves its layer via `spatial.layerFqOf(priorFocusedFq)`. If the inspector were holding focus, the resolved layer would be `/window/inspector` and pills would only land on inspector field zones. Instead, pills land on board / column / card scopes — i.e. `priorFocusedFq` was on `/window`, so the inspector layer never actually owned focus.
+
+2. **Escape is "problematic" inside the inspector.** Unlike `<JumpToOverlay>` and `<CommandPalette>`, the inspector layer does NOT install a sentinel `<FocusScope>` with an `app.dismiss` shadow command. (Compare `kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.tsx:242-275` where the jump-to layer wraps its body in a sentinel scope whose `commands` registers `app.dismiss` → `handleDismiss`.) Today, `inspectors-container.tsx:249-253` is just `<FocusLayer name=INSPECTOR_LAYER_NAME>{panelNodes}</FocusLayer>`. With nothing pinning `app.dismiss` at the inspector layer root, Escape's fall-through behavior depends on whether focus is actually inside the layer; with focus leaking to base, the drill-out chain walks the wrong tree.
+
+3. **Arrow-key navigation walks out of the inspector and visibly focuses cards on the base layer.** The kernel's snapshot is layer-scoped (`swissarmyhammer-focus/src/snapshot.rs:86-94`: `pub layer_fq: FullyQualifiedMoniker; pub scopes: Vec<SnapshotScope>`), and `buildSnapshotForFocused` in `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx:536-548` picks whichever layer registry `has` the focused FQM. So the only way arrow keys can move focus from the inspector body to a card on the board is if the focused FQM is already on `/window` — which is exactly what symptom (1) implies.
+
+### Likely root causes (investigate, don't assume)
+
+- **The entity wrap is `<FocusScope>`, not `<FocusZone>`.** `inspectors-container.tsx:419` wraps each panel body in `<FocusScope moniker={entityZoneSegment}>`. The doc-comment block in the same file (lines 80-98) says it should be `<FocusZone>`, and `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx:50-68` documents a "scope-is-leaf invariant" — registering further scopes/zones underneath logs `scope-not-leaf`. Field rows DO register their own zones inside this wrap, so the kernel may be treating the field tree as leaks under a leaf, which would explain why first-field focus never sticks to the inspector layer.
+- **No sentinel `app.dismiss` shadow at the inspector layer root.** Even if focus were inside the layer, drill-out has no terminal handler at the inspector edge. The jump-to and palette layers both install one and rely on it for clean dismissal; the inspector layer should follow the same pattern (dispatch `ui.inspector.close` on the inspector layer's `app.dismiss`, not on every panel's `<SlidePanel>` close button).
+- **`useFirstFieldFocus` in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-inspector.tsx:122-149` may be losing the race.** It calls `setFocus(firstFieldFq)` on mount, but the field zones it points at have to be registered with `LayerScopeRegistry` before `buildSnapshotForFocused` can find them. If the snapshot is built before any field zone has registered, `spatial_focus` is invoked with `snapshot: undefined` and the kernel rejects the claim (`state.rs:226-236`: `let layer = registry.layer(&snapshot.layer_fq)?;` returns `None`). The store stays at the previous focus (a card on the base layer), and the inspector layer is mounted without focus.
+
+### Files to read / modify
+
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.tsx` — layer mount + entity wrap + close commands.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-inspector.tsx` — `useFirstFieldFocus`.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.tsx` — sentinel pattern to mirror for the inspector.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx` / `focus-zone.tsx` — confirm zone vs scope semantics for an entity-keyed wrap that contains nested zones.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx` — `buildSnapshotForFocused`, `enumerateScopesInLayer`, `layerFqOf`.
+- `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/snapshot.rs`, `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/state.rs` — layer-scoped snapshot contract.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.guards.node.test.ts` — current source-level guards (note: the "permits a single FocusScope" guard may need to relax to `FocusZone` once the wrap is corrected).
+
+### Approach
+
+1. Reproduce the bug with a Playwright/Vitest browser test that opens an inspector panel, asserts the focused FQM is under `/window/inspector`, then presses ArrowLeft/ArrowDown and asserts focus stays under the inspector layer (no `/window/board/...` FQM).
+2. Confirm via the focus-debug overlay (toggle in dev) which layer actually owns focus immediately after opening a panel — that diagnostic alone tells you whether the bug is "claim never lands" or "claim lands but is later overridden".
+3. Fix root cause(s):
+   - Swap the entity wrap from `<FocusScope>` to `<FocusZone>` (matching the file's own doc comment) so nested field zones don't violate the leaf invariant.
+   - Add a sentinel `<FocusScope>` inside the inspector `<FocusLayer>` registering an `app.dismiss` shadow that dispatches `ui.inspector.close` (mirror jump-to-overlay.tsx:242-275).
+   - Make `useFirstFieldFocus` defer the `setFocus` claim until the first field zone has registered with the layer's registry (e.g. via a microtask / layout-effect that polls `registry.has(firstFieldFq)`, or via a registration-event hook on `LayerScopeRegistry`).
+4. Update the `inspectors-container.guards.node.test.ts` source-level guards to match the corrected wrap shape (and pin a sentinel-scope guard so the dismiss path can't regress silently).
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] With at least one inspector panel open, `spatial.focusedFq()` returns an FQM whose path is under `/window/inspector` immediately after the panel mounts (verified by an automated test, NOT by manual inspection).
+- [ ] Pressing the Jump-To key (`s` in vim, `Mod+G` in cua/emacs) while a panel is open opens `<JumpToOverlay>` and the enumerated targets all carry FQMs under `/window/inspector` — NOT under `/window/board/*`. Asserted in a browser test against `data-testid="jump-to-overlay"` and the rendered pill scope FQMs.
+- [ ] Pressing ArrowLeft / ArrowRight / ArrowUp / ArrowDown inside an inspector field never moves focus to a scope under `/window/board` while the inspector is open. Asserted in a browser test that drives the keyboard and reads `spatial.focusedFq()` after each key.
+- [ ] Pressing Escape inside an inspector field dismisses the topmost panel via `ui.inspector.close`, with no intermediate focus moves to base-layer scopes. Asserted in a browser test (extend the existing `inspector.close-restores-focus.browser.test.tsx` patterns).
+- [ ] No `scope-not-leaf` warning is emitted for the inspector entity wrap during a panel-open / panel-close cycle (asserted by a test that captures `console.error`/`console.warn` and grep for the literal token).
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] New browser test `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.layer-containment.browser.test.tsx` that:
+  - opens an inspector panel,
+  - asserts the focused FQM starts under `/window/inspector`,
+  - drives Arrow keys and asserts focus stays under `/window/inspector`,
+  - opens Jump-To and asserts every rendered pill's FQM is under `/window/inspector`,
+  - presses Escape and asserts the panel closed and focus restored to the prior board scope.
+- [ ] New unit / source-level guard in `inspectors-container.guards.node.test.ts` pinning that the inspector layer wraps its body in a sentinel `<FocusScope>` whose `commands` array contains an `app.dismiss` entry (so a future refactor can't silently delete the dismiss shadow).
+- [ ] Existing `inspectors-container.test.tsx`, `inspector.close-restores-focus.browser.test.tsx`, and `spatial-nav-jump-to.spatial.test.tsx` continue to pass (regression coverage).
+- [ ] Run `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test -- inspectors-container && npm test -- jump-to` and confirm green.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` — write the failing layer-containment browser test first (it should fail today by showing focus on `/window/board/...`), then implement the fix(es) until it passes.
+- Resist the urge to fix all three symptoms with three independent patches without first verifying which root cause the failing test pins. Investigate via the focus-debug overlay before patching.
\ No newline at end of file

changes.value: Inspector layer fails to contain focus: `s`-jump marks the base layer, Escape leaks, arrow-nav escapes

## What

The inspector overlay is supposed to act as a modal focus layer (`name="inspector"` mounted in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.tsx:250`), but in practice focus is leaking out to the parent `window` layer while at least one inspector panel is open. Three observable symptoms confirm this:

1. **Jump-To paints pills on the base layer.** Pressing `s` (vim) or `Mod+G` (cua/emacs) opens `<JumpToOverlay>`, which enumerates scopes via `useJumpTargets` in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.tsx:524-566`. That hook takes `priorFocusedFq` and resolves its layer via `spatial.layerFqOf(priorFocusedFq)`. If the inspector were holding focus, the resolved layer would be `/window/inspector` and pills would only land on inspector field zones. Instead, pills land on board / column / card scopes — i.e. `priorFocusedFq` was on `/window`, so the inspector layer never actually owned focus.

2. **Escape is "problematic" inside the inspector.** Unlike `<JumpToOverlay>` and `<CommandPalette>`, the inspector layer does NOT install a sentinel `<FocusScope>` with an `app.dismiss` shadow command. (Compare `kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.tsx:242-275` where the jump-to layer wraps its body in a sentinel scope whose `commands` registers `app.dismiss` → `handleDismiss`.) Today, `inspectors-container.tsx:249-253` is just `<FocusLayer name=INSPECTOR_LAYER_NAME>{panelNodes}</FocusLayer>`. With nothing pinning `app.dismiss` at the inspector layer root, Escape's fall-through behavior depends on whether focus is actually inside the layer; with focus leaking to base, the drill-out chain walks the wrong tree.

3. **Arrow-key navigation walks out of the inspector and visibly focuses cards on the base layer.** The kernel's snapshot is layer-scoped (`swissarmyhammer-focus/src/snapshot.rs:86-94`: `pub layer_fq: FullyQualifiedMoniker; pub scopes: Vec<SnapshotScope>`), and `buildSnapshotForFocused` in `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx:536-548` picks whichever layer registry `has` the focused FQM. So the only way arrow keys can move focus from the inspector body to a card on the board is if the focused FQM is already on `/window` — which is exactly what symptom (1) implies.

### Likely root causes (investigate, don't assume)

- **The entity wrap is `<FocusScope>`, not `<FocusZone>`.** `inspectors-container.tsx:419` wraps each panel body in `<FocusScope moniker={entityZoneSegment}>`. The doc-comment block in the same file (lines 80-98) says it should be `<FocusZone>`, and `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx:50-68` documents a "scope-is-leaf invariant" — registering further scopes/zones underneath logs `scope-not-leaf`. Field rows DO register their own zones inside this wrap, so the kernel may be treating the field tree as leaks under a leaf, which would explain why first-field focus never sticks to the inspector layer.
- **No sentinel `app.dismiss` shadow at the inspector layer root.** Even if focus were inside the layer, drill-out has no terminal handler at the inspector edge. The jump-to and palette layers both install one and rely on it for clean dismissal; the inspector layer should follow the same pattern (dispatch `ui.inspector.close` on the inspector layer's `app.dismiss`, not on every panel's `<SlidePanel>` close button).
- **`useFirstFieldFocus` in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-inspector.tsx:122-149` may be losing the race.** It calls `setFocus(firstFieldFq)` on mount, but the field zones it points at have to be registered with `LayerScopeRegistry` before `buildSnapshotForFocused` can find them. If the snapshot is built before any field zone has registered, `spatial_focus` is invoked with `snapshot: undefined` and the kernel rejects the claim (`state.rs:226-236`: `let layer = registry.layer(&snapshot.layer_fq)?;` returns `None`). The store stays at the previous focus (a card on the base layer), and the inspector layer is mounted without focus.

### Files to read / modify

- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.tsx` — layer mount + entity wrap + close commands.
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-inspector.tsx` — `useFirstFieldFocus`.
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.tsx` — sentinel pattern to mirror for the inspector.
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx` / `focus-zone.tsx` — confirm zone vs scope semantics for an entity-keyed wrap that contains nested zones.
- `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx` — `buildSnapshotForFocused`, `enumerateScopesInLayer`, `layerFqOf`.
- `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/snapshot.rs`, `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/state.rs` — layer-scoped snapshot contract.
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.guards.node.test.ts` — current source-level guards (note: the "permits a single FocusScope" guard may need to relax to `FocusZone` once the wrap is corrected).

### Approach

1. Reproduce the bug with a Playwright/Vitest browser test that opens an inspector panel, asserts the focused FQM is under `/window/inspector`, then presses ArrowLeft/ArrowDown and asserts focus stays under the inspector layer (no `/window/board/...` FQM).
2. Confirm via the focus-debug overlay (toggle in dev) which layer actually owns focus immediately after opening a panel — that diagnostic alone tells you whether the bug is "claim never lands" or "claim lands but is later overridden".
3. Fix root cause(s):
   - Swap the entity wrap from `<FocusScope>` to `<FocusZone>` (matching the file's own doc comment) so nested field zones don't violate the leaf invariant.
   - Add a sentinel `<FocusScope>` inside the inspector `<FocusLayer>` registering an `app.dismiss` shadow that dispatches `ui.inspector.close` (mirror jump-to-overlay.tsx:242-275).
   - Make `useFirstFieldFocus` defer the `setFocus` claim until the first field zone has registered with the layer's registry (e.g. via a microtask / layout-effect that polls `registry.has(firstFieldFq)`, or via a registration-event hook on `LayerScopeRegistry`).
4. Update the `inspectors-container.guards.node.test.ts` source-level guards to match the corrected wrap shape (and pin a sentinel-scope guard so the dismiss path can't regress silently).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] With at least one inspector panel open, `spatial.focusedFq()` returns an FQM whose path is under `/window/inspector` immediately after the panel mounts (verified by an automated test, NOT by manual inspection).
- [ ] Pressing the Jump-To key (`s` in vim, `Mod+G` in cua/emacs) while a panel is open opens `<JumpToOverlay>` and the enumerated targets all carry FQMs under `/window/inspector` — NOT under `/window/board/*`. Asserted in a browser test against `data-testid="jump-to-overlay"` and the rendered pill scope FQMs.
- [ ] Pressing ArrowLeft / ArrowRight / ArrowUp / ArrowDown inside an inspector field never moves focus to a scope under `/window/board` while the inspector is open. Asserted in a browser test that drives the keyboard and reads `spatial.focusedFq()` after each key.
- [ ] Pressing Escape inside an inspector field dismisses the topmost panel via `ui.inspector.close`, with no intermediate focus moves to base-layer scopes. Asserted in a browser test (extend the existing `inspector.close-restores-focus.browser.test.tsx` patterns).
- [ ] No `scope-not-leaf` warning is emitted for the inspector entity wrap during a panel-open / panel-close cycle (asserted by a test that captures `console.error`/`console.warn` and grep for the literal token).

## Tests

- [ ] New browser test `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.layer-containment.browser.test.tsx` that:
  - opens an inspector panel,
  - asserts the focused FQM starts under `/window/inspector`,
  - drives Arrow keys and asserts focus stays under `/window/inspector`,
  - opens Jump-To and asserts every rendered pill's FQM is under `/window/inspector`,
  - presses Escape and asserts the panel closed and focus restored to the prior board scope.
- [ ] New unit / source-level guard in `inspectors-container.guards.node.test.ts` pinning that the inspector layer wraps its body in a sentinel `<FocusScope>` whose `commands` array contains an `app.dismiss` entry (so a future refactor can't silently delete the dismiss shadow).
- [ ] Existing `inspectors-container.test.tsx`, `inspector.close-restores-focus.browser.test.tsx`, and `spatial-nav-jump-to.spatial.test.tsx` continue to pass (regression coverage).
- [ ] Run `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test -- inspectors-container && npm test -- jump-to` and confirm green.

## Workflow

- Use `/tdd` — write the failing layer-containment browser test first (it should fail today by showing focus on `/window/board/...`), then implement the fix(es) until it passes.
- Resist the urge to fix all three symptoms with three independent patches without first verifying which root cause the failing test pins. Investigate via the focus-debug overlay before patching.

-The inspector overlay is supposed to act as a modal focus layer (`name="inspector"` mounted in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.tsx:250`), but in practice focus is leaking out to the parent `window` layer while at least one inspector panel is open. Three observable symptoms confirm this:
+The inspector overlay is supposed to act as a modal focus boundary, but in practice focus leaks out to the parent `window` layer while at least one inspector panel is open. Three observable symptoms confirm this:

-1. **Jump-To paints pills on the base layer.** Pressing `s` (vim) or `Mod+G` (cua/emacs) opens `<JumpToOverlay>`, which enumerates scopes via `useJumpTargets` in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.tsx:524-566`. That hook takes `priorFocusedFq` and resolves its layer via `spatial.layerFqOf(priorFocusedFq)`. If the inspector were holding focus, the resolved layer would be `/window/inspector` and pills would only land on inspector field zones. Instead, pills land on board / column / card scopes — i.e. `priorFocusedFq` was on `/window`, so the inspector layer never actually owned focus.
+1. **Jump-To paints pills on the base layer.** Pressing `s` (vim) or `Mod+G` (cua/emacs) opens `<JumpToOverlay>`, which enumerates scopes via `useJumpTargets` (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.tsx:524-566`). That hook resolves the layer of `priorFocusedFq` via `spatial.layerFqOf(priorFocusedFq)` and calls `enumerateScopesInLayer(...)`. If the inspector were holding focus, the resolved layer would be `/window/inspector/...` and pills would only land on inspector field scopes. Instead, pills land on board / column / card scopes — i.e. `priorFocusedFq` was on `/window`.

-2. **Escape is "problematic" inside the inspector.** Unlike `<JumpToOverlay>` and `<CommandPalette>`, the inspector layer does NOT install a sentinel `<FocusScope>` with an `app.dismiss` shadow command. (Compare `kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.tsx:242-275` where the jump-to layer wraps its body in a sentinel scope whose `commands` registers `app.dismiss` → `handleDismiss`.) Today, `inspectors-container.tsx:249-253` is just `<FocusLayer name=INSPECTOR_LAYER_NAME>{panelNodes}</FocusLayer>`. With nothing pinning `app.dismiss` at the inspector layer root, Escape's fall-through behavior depends on whether focus is actually inside the layer; with focus leaking to base, the drill-out chain walks the wrong tree.
+2. **Escape is "problematic" inside the inspector.** Drill-out walks the focused scope's parent_zone chain inside the snapshot's layer; if focus is on a card on `/window` (not on a field inside `/window/inspector`), `nav.drillOut` walks the wrong tree.

-3. **Arrow-key navigation walks out of the inspector and visibly focuses cards on the base layer.** The kernel's snapshot is layer-scoped (`swissarmyhammer-focus/src/snapshot.rs:86-94`: `pub layer_fq: FullyQualifiedMoniker; pub scopes: Vec<SnapshotScope>`), and `buildSnapshotForFocused` in `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx:536-548` picks whichever layer registry `has` the focused FQM. So the only way arrow keys can move focus from the inspector body to a card on the board is if the focused FQM is already on `/window` — which is exactly what symptom (1) implies.
+3. **Arrow-key navigation walks out of the inspector and visibly focuses cards on the base layer.** The kernel's snapshot is layer-scoped (`swissarmyhammer-focus/src/snapshot.rs:86-94`: `pub layer_fq: FullyQualifiedMoniker; pub scopes: Vec<SnapshotScope>`), and `buildSnapshotForFocused` in `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx:536-548` picks whichever layer registry `has` the focused FQM. Arrow-key nav from the inspector body to a card on the board can only happen if the focused FQM is on `/window` — which is exactly what symptom (1) implies.

-### Likely root causes (investigate, don't assume)
+## Root cause
+
+`<FocusZone>` has been removed from the codebase (no `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-zone.*` file, no exported `FocusZone` symbol). With the container primitive gone, the two remaining primitives are:
+
+- `<FocusLayer>` — modal containment boundary (kernel pushes/pops a layer; navigation never crosses).
+- `<FocusScope>` — leaf in the spatial graph (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx:50-68` documents the "scope-is-leaf invariant" — registering further scopes underneath logs `scope-not-leaf`).

-- **The entity wrap is `<FocusScope>`, not `<FocusZone>`.** `inspectors-container.tsx:419` wraps each panel body in `<FocusScope moniker={entityZoneSegment}>`. The doc-comment block in the same file (lines 80-98) says it should be `<FocusZone>`, and `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx:50-68` documents a "scope-is-leaf invariant" — registering further scopes/zones underneath logs `scope-not-leaf`. Field rows DO register their own zones inside this wrap, so the kernel may be treating the field tree as leaks under a leaf, which would explain why first-field focus never sticks to the inspector layer.
-- **No sentinel `app.dismiss` shadow at the inspector layer root.** Even if focus were inside the layer, drill-out has no terminal handler at the inspector edge. The jump-to and palette layers both install one and rely on it for clean dismissal; the inspector layer should follow the same pattern (dispatch `ui.inspector.close` on the inspector layer's `app.dismiss`, not on every panel's `<SlidePanel>` close button).
-- **`useFirstFieldFocus` in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-inspector.tsx:122-149` may be losing the race.** It calls `setFocus(firstFieldFq)` on mount, but the field zones it points at have to be registered with `LayerScopeRegistry` before `buildSnapshotForFocused` can find them. If the snapshot is built before any field zone has registered, `spatial_focus` is invoked with `snapshot: undefined` and the kernel rejects the claim (`state.rs:226-236`: `let layer = registry.layer(&snapshot.layer_fq)?;` returns `None`). The store stays at the previous focus (a card on the base layer), and the inspector layer is mounted without focus.
+Today `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.tsx:419` wraps each open panel body in `<FocusScope moniker={entityZoneSegment}>`. That scope is not a leaf — `<EntityInspector>` registers a field `<FocusScope>` per row underneath it. The kernel can't treat that wrapper as a real containment boundary because it isn't a layer; it's a leaf with rogue descendants. So the per-entity barrier the doc comment in `inspectors-container.tsx:80-98` describes ("Iter 0 of the kernel's beam-search cascade is confined to peers within the same entity") simply does not exist at runtime, and field focus claims regularly miss the inspector layer entirely (the prior board focus stays on `/window`).
+
+**Fix shape.** Each open inspector panel must be its own `<FocusLayer>`, nested inside the existing `<FocusLayer name="inspector">`. That gives each panel a real containment boundary the kernel honors:

-### Approach
+## Files to read / modify

+- [ ] Pressing Escape inside an inspector field dismisses the topmost panel via `ui.inspector.close` (popping that panel's layer; the kernel restores focus to the parent layer's `last_focused`).

 ## What

-The inspector overlay is supposed to act as a modal focus layer (`name="inspector"` mounted in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.tsx:250`), but in practice focus is leaking out to the parent `window` layer while at least one inspector panel is open. Three observable symptoms confirm this:
+The inspector overlay is supposed to act as a modal focus boundary, but in practice focus leaks out to the parent `window` layer while at least one inspector panel is open. Three observable symptoms confirm this:

-1. **Jump-To paints pills on the base layer.** Pressing `s` (vim) or `Mod+G` (cua/emacs) opens `<JumpToOverlay>`, which enumerates scopes via `useJumpTargets` in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.tsx:524-566`. That hook takes `priorFocusedFq` and resolves its layer via `spatial.layerFqOf(priorFocusedFq)`. If the inspector were holding focus, the resolved layer would be `/window/inspector` and pills would only land on inspector field zones. Instead, pills land on board / column / card scopes — i.e. `priorFocusedFq` was on `/window`, so the inspector layer never actually owned focus.
+1. **Jump-To paints pills on the base layer.** Pressing `s` (vim) or `Mod+G` (cua/emacs) opens `<JumpToOverlay>`, which enumerates scopes via `useJumpTargets` (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.tsx:524-566`). That hook resolves the layer of `priorFocusedFq` via `spatial.layerFqOf(priorFocusedFq)` and calls `enumerateScopesInLayer(...)`. If the inspector were holding focus, the resolved layer would be `/window/inspector/...` and pills would only land on inspector field scopes. Instead, pills land on board / column / card scopes — i.e. `priorFocusedFq` was on `/window`.

-2. **Escape is "problematic" inside the inspector.** Unlike `<JumpToOverlay>` and `<CommandPalette>`, the inspector layer does NOT install a sentinel `<FocusScope>` with an `app.dismiss` shadow command. (Compare `kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.tsx:242-275` where the jump-to layer wraps its body in a sentinel scope whose `commands` registers `app.dismiss` → `handleDismiss`.) Today, `inspectors-container.tsx:249-253` is just `<FocusLayer name=INSPECTOR_LAYER_NAME>{panelNodes}</FocusLayer>`. With nothing pinning `app.dismiss` at the inspector layer root, Escape's fall-through behavior depends on whether focus is actually inside the layer; with focus leaking to base, the drill-out chain walks the wrong tree.
+2. **Escape is "problematic" inside the inspector.** Drill-out walks the focused scope's parent_zone chain inside the snapshot's layer; if focus is on a card on `/window` (not on a field inside `/window/inspector`), `nav.drillOut` walks the wrong tree.

-3. **Arrow-key navigation walks out of the inspector and visibly focuses cards on the base layer.** The kernel's snapshot is layer-scoped (`swissarmyhammer-focus/src/snapshot.rs:86-94`: `pub layer_fq: FullyQualifiedMoniker; pub scopes: Vec<SnapshotScope>`), and `buildSnapshotForFocused` in `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx:536-548` picks whichever layer registry `has` the focused FQM. So the only way arrow keys can move focus from the inspector body to a card on the board is if the focused FQM is already on `/window` — which is exactly what symptom (1) implies.
+3. **Arrow-key navigation walks out of the inspector and visibly focuses cards on the base layer.** The kernel's snapshot is layer-scoped (`swissarmyhammer-focus/src/snapshot.rs:86-94`: `pub layer_fq: FullyQualifiedMoniker; pub scopes: Vec<SnapshotScope>`), and `buildSnapshotForFocused` in `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx:536-548` picks whichever layer registry `has` the focused FQM. Arrow-key nav from the inspector body to a card on the board can only happen if the focused FQM is on `/window` — which is exactly what symptom (1) implies.

-### Likely root causes (investigate, don't assume)
+## Root cause

-- **The entity wrap is `<FocusScope>`, not `<FocusZone>`.** `inspectors-container.tsx:419` wraps each panel body in `<FocusScope moniker={entityZoneSegment}>`. The doc-comment block in the same file (lines 80-98) says it should be `<FocusZone>`, and `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx:50-68` documents a "scope-is-leaf invariant" — registering further scopes/zones underneath logs `scope-not-leaf`. Field rows DO register their own zones inside this wrap, so the kernel may be treating the field tree as leaks under a leaf, which would explain why first-field focus never sticks to the inspector layer.
-- **No sentinel `app.dismiss` shadow at the inspector layer root.** Even if focus were inside the layer, drill-out has no terminal handler at the inspector edge. The jump-to and palette layers both install one and rely on it for clean dismissal; the inspector layer should follow the same pattern (dispatch `ui.inspector.close` on the inspector layer's `app.dismiss`, not on every panel's `<SlidePanel>` close button).
-- **`useFirstFieldFocus` in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-inspector.tsx:122-149` may be losing the race.** It calls `setFocus(firstFieldFq)` on mount, but the field zones it points at have to be registered with `LayerScopeRegistry` before `buildSnapshotForFocused` can find them. If the snapshot is built before any field zone has registered, `spatial_focus` is invoked with `snapshot: undefined` and the kernel rejects the claim (`state.rs:226-236`: `let layer = registry.layer(&snapshot.layer_fq)?;` returns `None`). The store stays at the previous focus (a card on the base layer), and the inspector layer is mounted without focus.
+`<FocusZone>` has been removed from the codebase (no `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-zone.*` file, no exported `FocusZone` symbol). With the container primitive gone, the two remaining primitives are:

-### Files to read / modify
+- `<FocusLayer>` — modal containment boundary (kernel pushes/pops a layer; navigation never crosses).
+- `<FocusScope>` — leaf in the spatial graph (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx:50-68` documents the "scope-is-leaf invariant" — registering further scopes underneath logs `scope-not-leaf`).
+
+Today `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.tsx:419` wraps each open panel body in `<FocusScope moniker={entityZoneSegment}>`. That scope is not a leaf — `<EntityInspector>` registers a field `<FocusScope>` per row underneath it. The kernel can't treat that wrapper as a real containment boundary because it isn't a layer; it's a leaf with rogue descendants. So the per-entity barrier the doc comment in `inspectors-container.tsx:80-98` describes ("Iter 0 of the kernel's beam-search cascade is confined to peers within the same entity") simply does not exist at runtime, and field focus claims regularly miss the inspector layer entirely (the prior board focus stays on `/window`).
+
+**Fix shape.** Each open inspector panel must be its own `<FocusLayer>`, nested inside the existing `<FocusLayer name="inspector">`. That gives each panel a real containment boundary the kernel honors:
+
+- The outer `<FocusLayer name="inspector" parentLayerFq={windowLayerFq}>` stays as the inspector tier (z-index, focus-debug overlay tier).
+- Inside, replace `<FocusScope moniker={entityZoneSegment}>{body}</FocusScope>` with `<FocusLayer name={entityZoneSegment} parentLayerFq={inspectorLayerFq}>{body}</FocusLayer>`. The layer's name segment IS the entity moniker (`task:T1`, `tag:bug`, etc.), so the FQM composes to `/window/inspector/task:T1` — same path the current scope produces, but now as a real layer with its own `LayerScopeRegistry`.
+- Field `<FocusScope>` rows inside register against THAT entity-panel layer's registry, so navigation, jump-to enumeration, and drill-out all operate within the panel.
+- `useFirstFieldFocus` in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-inspector.tsx:122-149` claims into a field that lives in the panel-layer's registry, so `buildSnapshotForFocused` finds it and the kernel commits the claim.
+- Stack semantics: opening a second panel pushes a sibling layer; the kernel's per-layer parent chain (inspector → window) plus the new panel-tier means drill-out from a deep field walks panel → inspector → window in that order. Closing the topmost panel pops its layer and emits `last_focused` for the parent, restoring focus correctly without any React-side `useRestoreFocus` hack.

-3. **Arrow-key navigation walks out of the inspector and visibly focuses cards on the base layer.** The kernel's snapshot is layer-scoped (`swissarmyhammer-focus/src/snapshot.rs:86-94`: `pub layer_fq: FullyQualifiedMoniker; pub scopes: Vec<SnapshotScope>`), and `buildSnapshotForFocused` in `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx:536-548` picks whichever layer registry `has` the focused FQM. Arrow-key nav from the inspector body to a card on the board can only happen if the focused FQM is on `/window` — which is exactly what symptom (1) implies.
+2. **Escape is "problematic" inside the inspector.** Unlike `<JumpToOverlay>` and `<CommandPalette>`, the inspector layer does NOT install a sentinel `<FocusScope>` with an `app.dismiss` shadow command. (Compare `kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.tsx:242-275` where the jump-to layer wraps its body in a sentinel scope whose `commands` registers `app.dismiss` → `handleDismiss`.) Today, `inspectors-container.tsx:249-253` is just `<FocusLayer name=INSPECTOR_LAYER_NAME>{panelNodes}</FocusLayer>`. With nothing pinning `app.dismiss` at the inspector layer root, Escape's fall-through behavior depends on whether focus is actually inside the layer; with focus leaking to base, the drill-out chain walks the wrong tree.

-## Root cause
+3. **Arrow-key navigation walks out of the inspector and visibly focuses cards on the base layer.** The kernel's snapshot is layer-scoped (`swissarmyhammer-focus/src/snapshot.rs:86-94`: `pub layer_fq: FullyQualifiedMoniker; pub scopes: Vec<SnapshotScope>`), and `buildSnapshotForFocused` in `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx:536-548` picks whichever layer registry `has` the focused FQM. So the only way arrow keys can move focus from the inspector body to a card on the board is if the focused FQM is already on `/window` — which is exactly what symptom (1) implies.

-`<FocusZone>` has been removed from the codebase (no `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-zone.*` file, no exported `FocusZone` symbol). With the container primitive gone, the two remaining primitives are:
+### Likely root causes (investigate, don't assume)

-- `<FocusLayer>` — modal containment boundary (kernel pushes/pops a layer; navigation never crosses).
-- `<FocusScope>` — leaf in the spatial graph (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx:50-68` documents the "scope-is-leaf invariant" — registering further scopes underneath logs `scope-not-leaf`).
+- **The entity wrap is `<FocusScope>`, not `<FocusZone>`.** `inspectors-container.tsx:419` wraps each panel body in `<FocusScope moniker={entityZoneSegment}>`. The doc-comment block in the same file (lines 80-98) says it should be `<FocusZone>`, and `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx:50-68` documents a "scope-is-leaf invariant" — registering further scopes/zones underneath logs `scope-not-leaf`. Field rows DO register their own zones inside this wrap, so the kernel may be treating the field tree as leaks under a leaf, which would explain why first-field focus never sticks to the inspector layer.
+- **No sentinel `app.dismiss` shadow at the inspector layer root.** Even if focus were inside the layer, drill-out has no terminal handler at the inspector edge. The jump-to and palette layers both install one and rely on it for clean dismissal; the inspector layer should follow the same pattern (dispatch `ui.inspector.close` on the inspector layer's `app.dismiss`, not on every panel's `<SlidePanel>` close button).
+- **`useFirstFieldFocus` in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-inspector.tsx:122-149` may be losing the race.** It calls `setFocus(firstFieldFq)` on mount, but the field zones it points at have to be registered with `LayerScopeRegistry` before `buildSnapshotForFocused` can find them. If the snapshot is built before any field zone has registered, `spatial_focus` is invoked with `snapshot: undefined` and the kernel rejects the claim (`state.rs:226-236`: `let layer = registry.layer(&snapshot.layer_fq)?;` returns `None`). The store stays at the previous focus (a card on the base layer), and the inspector layer is mounted without focus.

-Today `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.tsx:419` wraps each open panel body in `<FocusScope moniker={entityZoneSegment}>`. That scope is not a leaf — `<EntityInspector>` registers a field `<FocusScope>` per row underneath it. The kernel can't treat that wrapper as a real containment boundary because it isn't a layer; it's a leaf with rogue descendants. So the per-entity barrier the doc comment in `inspectors-container.tsx:80-98` describes ("Iter 0 of the kernel's beam-search cascade is confined to peers within the same entity") simply does not exist at runtime, and field focus claims regularly miss the inspector layer entirely (the prior board focus stays on `/window`).
+### Files to read / modify

-**Fix shape.** Each open inspector panel must be its own `<FocusLayer>`, nested inside the existing `<FocusLayer name="inspector">`. That gives each panel a real containment boundary the kernel honors:
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.tsx` — layer mount + entity wrap + close commands.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-inspector.tsx` — `useFirstFieldFocus`.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.tsx` — sentinel pattern to mirror for the inspector.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx` / `focus-zone.tsx` — confirm zone vs scope semantics for an entity-keyed wrap that contains nested zones.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx` — `buildSnapshotForFocused`, `enumerateScopesInLayer`, `layerFqOf`.
+- `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/snapshot.rs`, `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/state.rs` — layer-scoped snapshot contract.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.guards.node.test.ts` — current source-level guards (note: the "permits a single FocusScope" guard may need to relax to `FocusZone` once the wrap is corrected).

-- The outer `<FocusLayer name="inspector" parentLayerFq={windowLayerFq}>` stays as the inspector tier (z-index, focus-debug overlay tier).
-- Inside, replace `<FocusScope moniker={entityZoneSegment}>{body}</FocusScope>` with `<FocusLayer name={entityZoneSegment} parentLayerFq={inspectorLayerFq}>{body}</FocusLayer>`. The layer's name segment IS the entity moniker (`task:T1`, `tag:bug`, etc.), so the FQM composes to `/window/inspector/task:T1` — same path the current scope produces, but now as a real layer with its own `LayerScopeRegistry`.
-- Field `<FocusScope>` rows inside register against THAT entity-panel layer's registry, so navigation, jump-to enumeration, and drill-out all operate within the panel.
-- `useFirstFieldFocus` in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-inspector.tsx:122-149` claims into a field that lives in the panel-layer's registry, so `buildSnapshotForFocused` finds it and the kernel commits the claim.
-- Stack semantics: opening a second panel pushes a sibling layer; the kernel's per-layer parent chain (inspector → window) plus the new panel-tier means drill-out from a deep field walks panel → inspector → window in that order. Closing the topmost panel pops its layer and emits `last_focused` for the parent, restoring focus correctly without any React-side `useRestoreFocus` hack.
+### Approach

-The `FocusLayer` push/pop machinery (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-layer.tsx:176-237`, `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/state.rs`) already supports nested layers under a non-window parent — `parentLayerFq` is the explicit prop for exactly this case (the palette and inspector layers already use it).
+1. Reproduce the bug with a Playwright/Vitest browser test that opens an inspector panel, asserts the focused FQM is under `/window/inspector`, then presses ArrowLeft/ArrowDown and asserts focus stays under the inspector layer (no `/window/board/...` FQM).
+2. Confirm via the focus-debug overlay (toggle in dev) which layer actually owns focus immediately after opening a panel — that diagnostic alone tells you whether the bug is "claim never lands" or "claim lands but is later overridden".
+3. Fix root cause(s):
+   - Swap the entity wrap from `<FocusScope>` to `<FocusZone>` (matching the file's own doc comment) so nested field zones don't violate the leaf invariant.
+   - Add a sentinel `<FocusScope>` inside the inspector `<FocusLayer>` registering an `app.dismiss` shadow that dispatches `ui.inspector.close` (mirror jump-to-overlay.tsx:242-275).
+   - Make `useFirstFieldFocus` defer the `setFocus` claim until the first field zone has registered with the layer's registry (e.g. via a microtask / layout-effect that polls `registry.has(firstFieldFq)`, or via a registration-event hook on `LayerScopeRegistry`).
+4. Update the `inspectors-container.guards.node.test.ts` source-level guards to match the corrected wrap shape (and pin a sentinel-scope guard so the dismiss path can't regress silently).

+## Review Findings (2026-05-09)
+
+Reviewed by reviewer subagent. Load-bearing fix is correct and well-tested. Three findings, all minor; recommend a small follow-up pass before moving to done.
+
+### Finding 1 — Acceptance-criterion drift not formally amended (medium)
+
+The criterion "Opening a second panel … arrow-keys cross between the two panels via the inspector layer's iter-1 escalation" is **incompatible with the modal-layer contract** the rest of this card establishes. Verified against the kernel:
+
+- `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/snapshot.rs` — `NavSnapshot { layer_fq, scopes }` is strictly single-layer; `IndexedSnapshot::parent_zone_chain` walks within one snapshot only.
+- `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/state.rs::navigate` runs `pick_target` against a single `IndexedSnapshot` — no sibling-layer escalation primitive exists.
+- `state.rs::resolve_fallback` Phase 2 walks parent layers ONLY for focus-loss restoration, and even then only commits a target that is also present in the lost layer's snapshot. There is no analogue for cardinal-arrow navigation.
+
+The implementer correctly chose strict containment over the (impossible) iter-1 cross-panel nav and pinned that choice in `inspector.cross-panel-nav.browser.test.tsx` (rewritten to assert the layer fence blocks ArrowLeft/Right/Up/Down between sibling panels). That is the right call, but the acceptance criterion above remains worded as if cross-panel arrow nav still works — the user's review brief explicitly required the criterion be either satisfied as-stated OR formally amended with rationale.
+
+**Action:** Replace the bullet with an amended version, e.g.: "Opening a second panel while the first is still open creates a sibling layer under `/window/inspector` (not nested inside the first panel's layer). Cardinal arrow keys are confined to the source panel by the per-panel layer fence — cross-panel navigation lives on the palette / jump-to / mouse, not on cardinal arrows. (Amended from the original wording, which mentioned iter-1 escalation; the kernel's `NavSnapshot` is single-layer and offers no sibling-layer escalation primitive — `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/snapshot.rs:86-94`, `state.rs::navigate`.)"
+
+### Finding 2 — Two acceptance-criterion test gaps (low)
+
+The structural fix makes both criteria true by construction, but the criteria explicitly required asserted guards and neither landed:
+
+- **`scope-not-leaf` warning capture missing.** Criterion: "No `scope-not-leaf` warning is emitted during a panel-open / panel-close cycle (asserted by capturing `console.error` / `console.warn` and grepping for the literal token)." No such capture exists in `inspectors-container.layer-containment.browser.test.tsx` or any other updated/new test. (`scope-not-leaf` is emitted only by `focus-scope.tsx` / `data-table.tsx` / `fields/field.tsx`, none of which are direct children of the new panel layer, so structurally clean — but the asserted guard the criterion called for is missing.)
+- **Jump-To enumeration test missing.** Criterion: "Pressing `s` (vim) / `Mod+G` (cua/emacs) while a panel is open opens `<JumpToOverlay>` and every enumerated target FQM lives under that panel's layer — NOT under `/window/board/*`." The new browser test exercises arrow keys, focus FQM, and panel pop/close, but does not exercise Jump-To. `spatial-nav-jump-to.spatial.test.tsx` does not appear updated for the new shape either.
+
+**Action:** Add a `console.warn` / `console.error` capture in `inspectors-container.layer-containment.browser.test.tsx`'s panel-open / panel-close cycle test, and add a Jump-To enumeration assertion (open the panel, fire the bind, assert every enumerated target FQM has the panel-layer prefix).
+
+### Finding 3 — Stale doc comment in `entity-inspector.tsx` (low)
+
+`entity-inspector.tsx` (lines around 169-171) still documents `<Field>`'s focus participation as: "the unified cascade drives keyboard navigation between rows (iter 1 escalates to the parent zone and lands on the neighbouring field zone)". With the panel now being a `<FocusLayer>` (not a scope), field zones register at the panel-layer root with `parentZone === null` (asserted in the new test). There is no parent zone to escalate to; cardinal nav resolves entirely within the panel-layer's flat scope list via beam search. The comment now misleads readers about the mechanism.
+
+**Action:** Update the comment to reflect that field zones live at the panel-layer root and cardinal nav resolves via beam search inside the panel's `LayerScopeRegistry` (no parent-zone escalation needed).
+
+### Things that look right
+
+- `useFullyQualifiedMoniker()` is read from the correct context: `<InspectorsContainer>` reads `/window` (outside the inspector layer), `<InspectorPanel>` reads `/window/inspector` (inside the inspector layer's `FullyQualifiedMonikerContext.Provider`). No accidental window-FQM bug.
+- `asSegment(\`${entityType}:${entityId}\`)` uses the project's standard branding helper — no unsound `as` casts.
+- The new layer-containment test is real (uses `installKernelSimulator`, asserts `spatial_push_layer` history and parent linkage, FQM composition `/window/inspector/task:TA`, sibling-layer push semantics, panel-layer pop on close).
+- `inspectors-container.guards.node.test.ts` correctly forbids `\bFocusScope\b` in code and pins the new `parentLayerFq={inspectorLayerFq}` wiring.
+- The first-field focus claim is now structurally guaranteed to land inside the panel layer and the new test asserts `focusedFq.startsWith("/window/inspector/task:TA/")` — the implementer pinned the fix rather than relying on it.

 - `asSegment(\`${entityType}:${entityId}\`)` uses the project's standard branding helper — no unsound `as` casts.

+- The first-field focus claim is now structurally guaranteed to land inside the panel layer and the new test asserts `focusedFq.startsWith("/window/inspector/task:TA/")` — the implementer pinned the fix rather than relying on it.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-05-10) — Production bug persists
+
+**REOPENED.** The structural fix landed and the kernel-simulator tests pass, but in the running app the bug is unchanged: opening an inspector while a card on the board has focus does NOT claim focus into the inspector panel. The user can still navigate cards on the board with arrow keys while the inspector is visible, and pressing `s` paints Jump-To pills on the board — exactly the symptoms the original task described.
+
+This means the green tests are pinning a fictional flow. The kernel-simulator path that the new `inspectors-container.layer-containment.browser.test.tsx` exercises does not match the production focus-claim flow.
+
+### What to investigate (do this with the focus-debug overlay enabled live)
+
+1. **Does `useFirstFieldFocus` actually fire?** `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-inspector.tsx:122-149`. The effect has `[firstFieldFq]` deps. `firstFieldFq` is `undefined` until the schema loads (`getSchema(entity.entity_type)?.entity` returns undefined while loading). The effect's first run returns early on `if (!firstFieldFq) return;` and the cleanup never runs. When the schema loads later, `firstFieldFq` becomes defined and the effect fires — but does it ever, for every entity type? Add explicit console logging or a temp `data-attr` you can grep.
+
+2. **Does `setFocus(firstFieldFq)` actually commit, or is the kernel rejecting the snapshot?** `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/entity-focus-context.tsx:371-398` calls `spatial.focus(fq)` then `.catch(error => console.error("spatial_focus failed:", error))`. Check the dev console — is "spatial_focus failed" being logged? If yes, the kernel is rejecting the claim. The most likely cause: `buildSnapshotForFocused` in `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx:536-548` couldn't find any registry that contains `firstFieldFq`, so the snapshot is `undefined` and the Rust `state::focus` returns `None` because `let layer = registry.layer(&snapshot.layer_fq)?;` short-circuits.
+
+3. **When `useFirstFieldFocus` runs, has the field zone been registered with the panel layer's `LayerScopeRegistry` yet?** This is a layout/timing question. The panel `<FocusLayer>` mounts → field `<FocusScope>` rows mount → each registers with the layer registry via a useEffect. `useFirstFieldFocus` is a useEffect on the EntityInspector — it may run BEFORE the field zones' registration effects complete. If so, the focus claim arrives before the registry has the FQM, and the snapshot is undefined.
+

Layers in this codebase typically don't claim focus directly — claims happen on a scope inside the layer — but the panel needs SOMETHING to land focus inside its boundary on mount.

+
+### Acceptance for re-fix
+
+- [ ] Open an inspector panel from a card-focused state — `spatial.focusedFq()` returns an FQM under `/window/inspector/<entity>/` within one frame of the panel mounting. Verified with a real end-to-end browser test, NOT a kernel-simulator test.
+- [ ] Pressing `s` (vim) immediately after opens Jump-To and pills paint on inspector field scopes, NOT on board scopes. Verified end-to-end.
+- [ ] Pressing arrow keys does NOT move focus on the board while the inspector is open. Verified end-to-end.
+- [ ] No `spatial_focus failed:` console.error is emitted during the panel-open lifecycle.
+- [ ] The new end-to-end test FAILS on `main` before this fix and PASSES after.

+- [ ] The new end-to-end test FAILS on `main` before this fix and PASSES after.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-05-10 08:59) — Iteration-3 render-time fix
+
+Re-reviewed by reviewer subagent after the implementer moved `<FocusLayer>` registration from `useEffect` to render-time (with a ref-guarded idempotency check) so descendant `useEffect`s see ancestor layers already pushed and registered. The fix is **correct and well-justified** — a render-time push is genuinely the only React mechanism that gives parent-before-child setup ordering (`useLayoutEffect` is also child-first; `useInsertionEffect` likewise). The new regression test is real, mounts the full app shell, and the strict-Rust-mirror invoke handler genuinely rejects undefined snapshots and missing-FQM scopes — the test would fail on `main` before the fix lands. Three soft findings; none are blockers.
+
+### Warnings
+
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-layer.tsx` (file-header docstring lines 22-30) — the "Lifecycle" section still describes the layer's behaviour as "Mount: ... invokes `spatial_push_layer` ... Unmount: invokes `spatial_pop_layer`" without flagging the unusual render-time invocation pattern. The detailed inline comment block at the render-time site (the "Eager top-down registration" block) explains it well, but a reader skimming the file's header docstring will miss the design choice and may try to "fix" the render-time push back to a `useEffect`. Update the lifecycle bullet to read e.g. "Push is dispatched during render (top-down) so descendant `useEffect`s see ancestor layers already populated; pop runs on unmount via a `useEffect` cleanup. See the 'Eager top-down registration' block below for the rationale." Why: the doc comment is the contract; future maintainers should not have to reverse-engineer the unusual pattern from the implementation.
+
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-layer.tsx` (the "Eager top-down registration" block, around the render-time `pushLayer` / `registerLayerRegistry` site) — render-time side effects are safe today only because the codebase contains no `<Suspense>`, `startTransition`, `useTransition`, or `useDeferredValue`. If a future change introduces any of those above a `<FocusLayer>` (or wraps a focus-state change in a transition), React may abandon a render after the side effects fire. Because the unmount cleanup lives in a `useEffect` that only attaches on commit, an abandoned render leaks a kernel-side `pushLayer` (and a `layerRegistriesRef` entry) that is never popped. Add a comment near the render-time block warning future maintainers, e.g. "WARNING: this pattern is safe only because the app does not use Suspense or transitions above any FocusLayer. Introducing either could cause render-abandonment to leak kernel-side layer pushes; migrate this back to a commit-time mechanism (or add a render-id tracking refcount) before adding such boundaries." Why: the invariant is real but invisible — a future PR adding `<Suspense>` for code-splitting could regress focus state in ways no one would attribute to this primitive.
+
+### Nits
+
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-layer.tsx` (the unmount-cleanup `useEffect` with empty deps and the `// eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` suppression) — the empty-deps + lint suppression combination is itself a smell that future readers may mistake for a bug. The current comment justifies it, but consider strengthening the explanation: explicitly note that the cleanup reads from `eagerRef.current` (always live) and `popLayer` (closure-captured but functionally stable across renders for our purposes), and that re-running the effect on dep changes would race with the render-time tear-down + re-publish path. The current wording ("re-running on every change would race") is accurate but terse.
+
+### Things that look right
+
+- The render-time push pattern is the **right mechanism** for parent-before-child setup ordering. `useLayoutEffect` and `useInsertionEffect` are both child-first; only render itself is top-down in React. The implementer correctly identified that the `useEffect`-based design was structurally unable to satisfy the ordering invariant, and chose the only React mechanism that can.
+- StrictMode safety is verified: the `eagerRef.current === null || eagerRef.current.fq !== fq` guard correctly skips the second render in StrictMode's intentional double-invocation. The simulated unmount-remount cycle correctly tears down (via the `useEffect` cleanup, which sets `eagerRef.current = null`) and re-publishes on the second mount. The pre-existing test "layer key is stable across re-renders" in `focus-layer.test.tsx` continues to pin this invariant (one push per re-render).
+- FQM-swap path: when `name` or `parentLayerFq` changes mid-life, the prior `unregister()` and `popLayer(prior fq)` run synchronously during render before the new push fires. Consistent with the kernel's expectations.
+- Multi-window safety holds: each Tauri window has its own React tree, its own `SpatialFocusProvider` (and thus its own `layerRegistriesRef` map), and the kernel's `window_label_from(&window)` derivation in `kanban-app/src/commands.rs:2415` keys per-window state separately. The render-time push composes the FQM from the window-segment so cross-window collisions are structurally impossible.
+- The new regression test (`inspector.first-field-focus-race.browser.test.tsx`) is **rigorous**: it mounts the full app shell (`<AppShell><InspectorsContainer /></AppShell>` inside the real provider stack), installs a strict-Rust-mirror invoke handler that genuinely rejects `snapshot: undefined`, missing-from-`layers`, and missing-from-`scopes`, and asserts both the kernel's `currentFocus.fq` advances into `/window/inspector/task:T1/` AND the IPC ordering (`push:task:T1` arrives before `focus:.../field:task:T1.title`). The test would fail on `main` (where the IPC dispatches `spatial_focus` with `snapshot: undefined`).
+- All earlier findings are addressed:
+  - Cross-panel-nav AC was amended in iteration 2.
+  - The Jump-To enumeration test in `inspectors-container.layer-containment.browser.test.tsx` (the test at "Jump-To enumeration from inside an open panel only sees scopes under the panel layer ...") closes the gap from finding 2 in the 2026-05-09 review.
+  - The `scope-not-leaf` warning capture test in the same file (the test at "emits no `scope-not-leaf` warning during a panel-open / panel-close cycle") closes the second gap from finding 2.
+  - The `entity-inspector.tsx` doc comment around lines 167-176 has been updated to reflect that field zones live at the panel-layer root with `parentZone === null` and that cardinal nav resolves via beam search inside the panel's `LayerScopeRegistry` — closes finding 3 from 2026-05-09.
+- `popLayer` IPC failure is benign on the kernel side — `kanban-app/src/commands.rs::spatial_pop_layer` calls `registry.remove_layer(&fq)`, which is `HashMap::remove` and a no-op on missing keys. So a transient `pushLayer` failure followed by a `popLayer` on unmount cannot wedge the kernel.
\ No newline at end of file

-## What
-
-The inspector overlay is supposed to act as a modal focus boundary, but in practice focus leaks out to the parent `window` layer while at least one inspector panel is open. Three observable symptoms confirm this:
+## Architecture (user-confirmed, HARD requirement)

-1. **Jump-To paints pills on the base layer.** Pressing `s` (vim) or `Mod+G` (cua/emacs) opens `<JumpToOverlay>`, which enumerates scopes via `useJumpTargets` (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.tsx:524-566`). That hook resolves the layer of `priorFocusedFq` via `spatial.layerFqOf(priorFocusedFq)` and calls `enumerateScopesInLayer(...)`. If the inspector were holding focus, the resolved layer would be `/window/inspector/...` and pills would only land on inspector field scopes. Instead, pills land on board / column / card scopes — i.e. `priorFocusedFq` was on `/window`.
+**There is ONE `<FocusLayer name="inspector">` for ALL open inspector panels.** Each panel is content inside that one shared layer — NOT its own layer. The inspector layer sits above the window layer. When the inspector layer is up, focus / jump / navigation must be confined to that single inspector layer.

-2. **Escape is "problematic" inside the inspector.** Drill-out walks the focused scope's parent_zone chain inside the snapshot's layer; if focus is on a card on `/window` (not on a field inside `/window/inspector`), `nav.drillOut` walks the wrong tree.
+The previous direction (one `<FocusLayer>` per panel) was wrong. Revert it.

-3. **Arrow-key navigation walks out of the inspector and visibly focuses cards on the base layer.** The kernel's snapshot is layer-scoped (`swissarmyhammer-focus/src/snapshot.rs:86-94`: `pub layer_fq: FullyQualifiedMoniker; pub scopes: Vec<SnapshotScope>`), and `buildSnapshotForFocused` in `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx:536-548` picks whichever layer registry `has` the focused FQM. Arrow-key nav from the inspector body to a card on the board can only happen if the focused FQM is on `/window` — which is exactly what symptom (1) implies.
+The iteration-3 render-time push pattern (added to `<FocusLayer>` itself) was solving a fictional problem caused by the per-panel split and has introduced regressions on the window root layer too. Revert it.

-## Root cause
-
-`<FocusZone>` has been removed from the codebase (no `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-zone.*` file, no exported `FocusZone` symbol). With the container primitive gone, the two remaining primitives are:
-
-- `<FocusLayer>` — modal containment boundary (kernel pushes/pops a layer; navigation never crosses).
-- `<FocusScope>` — leaf in the spatial graph (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx:50-68` documents the "scope-is-leaf invariant" — registering further scopes underneath logs `scope-not-leaf`).
-
-Today `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.tsx:419` wraps each open panel body in `<FocusScope moniker={entityZoneSegment}>`. That scope is not a leaf — `<EntityInspector>` registers a field `<FocusScope>` per row underneath it. The kernel can't treat that wrapper as a real containment boundary because it isn't a layer; it's a leaf with rogue descendants. So the per-entity barrier the doc comment in `inspectors-container.tsx:80-98` describes ("Iter 0 of the kernel's beam-search cascade is confined to peers within the same entity") simply does not exist at runtime, and field focus claims regularly miss the inspector layer entirely (the prior board focus stays on `/window`).
-
-**Fix shape.** Each open inspector panel must be its own `<FocusLayer>`, nested inside the existing `<FocusLayer name="inspector">`. That gives each panel a real containment boundary the kernel honors:
-
-- The outer `<FocusLayer name="inspector" parentLayerFq={windowLayerFq}>` stays as the inspector tier (z-index, focus-debug overlay tier).
-- Inside, replace `<FocusScope moniker={entityZoneSegment}>{body}</FocusScope>` with `<FocusLayer name={entityZoneSegment} parentLayerFq={inspectorLayerFq}>{body}</FocusLayer>`. The layer's name segment IS the entity moniker (`task:T1`, `tag:bug`, etc.), so the FQM composes to `/window/inspector/task:T1` — same path the current scope produces, but now as a real layer with its own `LayerScopeRegistry`.
-- Field `<FocusScope>` rows inside register against THAT entity-panel layer's registry, so navigation, jump-to enumeration, and drill-out all operate within the panel.
-- `useFirstFieldFocus` in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-inspector.tsx:122-149` claims into a field that lives in the panel-layer's registry, so `buildSnapshotForFocused` finds it and the kernel commits the claim.
-- Stack semantics: opening a second panel pushes a sibling layer; the kernel's per-layer parent chain (inspector → window) plus the new panel-tier means drill-out from a deep field walks panel → inspector → window in that order. Closing the topmost panel pops its layer and emits `last_focused` for the parent, restoring focus correctly without any React-side `useRestoreFocus` hack.
-
-The `FocusLayer` push/pop machinery (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-layer.tsx:176-237`, `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/state.rs`) already supports nested layers under a non-window parent — `parentLayerFq` is the explicit prop for exactly this case (the palette and inspector layers already use it).
-

+The original race (the real reason iteration 3 was reached for):
+- `<FocusLayer>`'s `registerLayerRegistry` runs in a `useEffect` at the layer level.
+- `<EntityInspector>`'s `useFirstFieldFocus` runs in a `useEffect` at a descendant level.
+- React fires effects child-first, so `useFirstFieldFocus` fires BEFORE the inspector layer's `registerLayerRegistry`.
+- When `useFirstFieldFocus` calls `setFocus(firstFieldFq)`, `buildSnapshotForFocused` walks `layerRegistriesRef` and finds no registry containing the field FQM (because the layer hasn't registered itself yet) → snapshot is `undefined` → kernel silently rejects → focus stays on prior board scope.

+- **(a)** Defer the `setFocus` call in `useFirstFieldFocus` (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-inspector.tsx:122-149`) by one tick so it runs AFTER all useEffects in the current commit have fired. Microtask (`queueMicrotask`) won't cross effect-flush boundaries; a `setTimeout(..., 0)` or `requestAnimationFrame` will. Pick the smallest delay that consistently works under StrictMode.
+- **(b)** Subscribe to the layer registry's registration event so the claim fires when the layer is added to `layerRegistriesRef`. This requires adding a small subscription primitive to `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx` (`SpatialFocusActions.subscribeLayerRegistered(layerFq, listener)` or similar).

(This is a smaller, safer change than the iter-3 render-time push because it only touches the React-side bookkeeping, not the IPC.)

-## Architecture (user-confirmed, HARD requirement)
+## What

-**There is ONE `<FocusLayer name="inspector">` for ALL open inspector panels.** Each panel is content inside that one shared layer — NOT its own layer. The inspector layer sits above the window layer. When the inspector layer is up, focus / jump / navigation must be confined to that single inspector layer.
+The inspector overlay is supposed to act as a modal focus boundary, but in practice focus leaks out to the parent `window` layer while at least one inspector panel is open. Three observable symptoms confirm this:

-The previous direction (one `<FocusLayer>` per panel) was wrong. Revert it.
+1. **Jump-To paints pills on the base layer.** Pressing `s` (vim) or `Mod+G` (cua/emacs) opens `<JumpToOverlay>`, which enumerates scopes via `useJumpTargets` (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.tsx:524-566`). That hook resolves the layer of `priorFocusedFq` via `spatial.layerFqOf(priorFocusedFq)` and calls `enumerateScopesInLayer(...)`. If the inspector were holding focus, the resolved layer would be `/window/inspector/...` and pills would only land on inspector field scopes. Instead, pills land on board / column / card scopes — i.e. `priorFocusedFq` was on `/window`.

-The iteration-3 render-time push pattern (added to `<FocusLayer>` itself) was solving a fictional problem caused by the per-panel split and has introduced regressions on the window root layer too. Revert it.
+2. **Escape is "problematic" inside the inspector.** Drill-out walks the focused scope's parent_zone chain inside the snapshot's layer; if focus is on a card on `/window` (not on a field inside `/window/inspector`), `nav.drillOut` walks the wrong tree.

-## Current production symptoms (confirmed by user)
+3. **Arrow-key navigation walks out of the inspector and visibly focuses cards on the base layer.** The kernel's snapshot is layer-scoped (`swissarmyhammer-focus/src/snapshot.rs:86-94`: `pub layer_fq: FullyQualifiedMoniker; pub scopes: Vec<SnapshotScope>`), and `buildSnapshotForFocused` in `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx:536-548` picks whichever layer registry `has` the focused FQM. Arrow-key nav from the inspector body to a card on the board can only happen if the focused FQM is on `/window` — which is exactly what symptom (1) implies.

-1. **No inspector open: pressing `s` shows zero jump labels.** Jump-To enumerates scopes in the layer that owns prior focus. With nothing focused on the window, enumeration falls back, but no pills paint. Root regression — this was working before iteration 3.
+## Root cause

-2. **Inspector open: focus does land in the inspector field after iteration 3** (arrow-key nav inside the panel works). That part of iteration 3 succeeded.
+`<FocusZone>` has been removed from the codebase (no `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-zone.*` file, no exported `FocusZone` symbol). With the container primitive gone, the two remaining primitives are:

-3. **Inspector open + press `s`: backdrop darkens the window, no pills appear.** Two stacked bugs:
-   - **Z-index**: `<JumpPill>` and the jump-to backdrop in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.tsx` have no `z-index`. The inspector panel uses `z-30` which paints above any z-auto sibling — so the backdrop only darkens the area outside the inspector panel and the pills paint at field coordinates but are hidden by the inspector panel above them. Confirmed by reading `jump-to-overlay.tsx:347-377` (chrome `<div data-testid="jump-to-overlay">` with no z-index, backdrop `fixed inset-0 ... bg-black/30`, pill `fixed left:rect.left+4 top:rect.top+4` — all z-auto).
-   - Even setting that aside, with NO inspector and pressing `s`, no pills paint, suggesting the iter-3 render-time push broke the window layer's registration timing too.
+- `<FocusLayer>` — modal containment boundary (kernel pushes/pops a layer; navigation never crosses).
+- `<FocusScope>` — leaf in the spatial graph (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx:50-68` documents the "scope-is-leaf invariant" — registering further scopes underneath logs `scope-not-leaf`).

-## Required fix shape
+Today `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.tsx:419` wraps each open panel body in `<FocusScope moniker={entityZoneSegment}>`. That scope is not a leaf — `<EntityInspector>` registers a field `<FocusScope>` per row underneath it. The kernel can't treat that wrapper as a real containment boundary because it isn't a layer; it's a leaf with rogue descendants. So the per-entity barrier the doc comment in `inspectors-container.tsx:80-98` describes ("Iter 0 of the kernel's beam-search cascade is confined to peers within the same entity") simply does not exist at runtime, and field focus claims regularly miss the inspector layer entirely (the prior board focus stays on `/window`).

-### Step 1 — revert iteration 1 (per-panel `<FocusLayer>`)
+**Fix shape.** Each open inspector panel must be its own `<FocusLayer>`, nested inside the existing `<FocusLayer name="inspector">`. That gives each panel a real containment boundary the kernel honors:

-`kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.tsx` currently wraps each open panel body in a NESTED `<FocusLayer name={entityLayerName} parentLayerFq={inspectorLayerFq}>`. -## What
-
-The inspector overlay is supposed to act as a modal focus boundary, but in practice focus leaks out to the parent `window` layer while at least one inspector panel is open. Three observable symptoms confirm this:
-
-1. **Jump-To paints pills on the base layer.** Pressing `s` (vim) or `Mod+G` (cua/emacs) opens `<JumpToOverlay>`, which enumerates scopes via `useJumpTargets` (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.tsx:524-566`). That hook resolves the layer of `priorFocusedFq` via `spatial.layerFqOf(priorFocusedFq)` and calls `enumerateScopesInLayer(...)`. If the inspector were holding focus, the resolved layer would be `/window/inspector/...` and pills would only land on inspector field scopes. Instead, pills land on board / column / card scopes — i.e. `priorFocusedFq` was on `/window`.
-
-2. **Escape is "problematic" inside the inspector.** Drill-out walks the focused scope's parent_zone chain inside the snapshot's layer; if focus is on a card on `/window` (not on a field inside `/window/inspector`), `nav.drillOut` walks the wrong tree.
-
-3. **Arrow-key navigation walks out of the inspector and visibly focuses cards on the base layer.** The kernel's snapshot is layer-scoped (`swissarmyhammer-focus/src/snapshot.rs:86-94`: `pub layer_fq: FullyQualifiedMoniker; pub scopes: Vec<SnapshotScope>`), and `buildSnapshotForFocused` in `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx:536-548` picks whichever layer registry `has` the focused FQM. Arrow-key nav from the inspector body to a card on the board can only happen if the focused FQM is on `/window` — which is exactly what symptom (1) implies.
-
-## Root cause
-
-`<FocusZone>` has been removed from the codebase (no `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-zone.*` file, no exported `FocusZone` symbol). With the container primitive gone, the two remaining primitives are:
-
-- `<FocusLayer>` — modal containment boundary (kernel pushes/pops a layer; navigation never crosses).
-- `<FocusScope>` — leaf in the spatial graph (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx:50-68` documents the "scope-is-leaf invariant" — registering further scopes underneath logs `scope-not-leaf`).
-
-Today `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.tsx:419` wraps each open panel body in `<FocusScope moniker={entityZoneSegment}>`. That scope is not a leaf — `<EntityInspector>` registers a field `<FocusScope>` per row underneath it. The kernel can't treat that wrapper as a real containment boundary because it isn't a layer; it's a leaf with rogue descendants. So the per-entity barrier the doc comment in `inspectors-container.tsx:80-98` describes ("Iter 0 of the kernel's beam-search cascade is confined to peers within the same entity") simply does not exist at runtime, and field focus claims regularly miss the inspector layer entirely (the prior board focus stays on `/window`).
-
-**Fix shape.** Each open inspector panel must be its own `<FocusLayer>`, nested inside the existing `<FocusLayer name="inspector">`. That gives each panel a real containment boundary the kernel honors:
-
-- The outer `<FocusLayer name="inspector" parentLayerFq={windowLayerFq}>` stays as the inspector tier (z-index, focus-debug overlay tier).
-- Inside, replace `<FocusScope moniker={entityZoneSegment}>{body}</FocusScope>` with `<FocusLayer name={entityZoneSegment} parentLayerFq={inspectorLayerFq}>{body}</FocusLayer>`. The layer's name segment IS the entity moniker (`task:T1`, `tag:bug`, etc.), so the FQM composes to `/window/inspector/task:T1` — same path the current scope produces, but now as a real layer with its own `LayerScopeRegistry`.
-- Field `<FocusScope>` rows inside register against THAT entity-panel layer's registry, so navigation, jump-to enumeration, and drill-out all operate within the panel.
-- `useFirstFieldFocus` in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-inspector.tsx:122-149` claims into a field that lives in the panel-layer's registry, so `buildSnapshotForFocused` finds it and the kernel commits the claim.
-- Stack semantics: opening a second panel pushes a sibling layer; the kernel's per-layer parent chain (inspector → window) plus the new panel-tier means drill-out from a deep field walks panel → inspector → window in that order. Closing the topmost panel pops its layer and emits `last_focused` for the parent, restoring focus correctly without any React-side `useRestoreFocus` hack.
-
-The `FocusLayer` push/pop machinery (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-layer.tsx:176-237`, `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/state.rs`) already supports nested layers under a non-window parent — `parentLayerFq` is the explicit prop for exactly this case (the palette and inspector layers already use it).
-
-## Files to read / modify
-
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.tsx` — replace the per-panel `<FocusScope>` wrap with a nested `<FocusLayer>` keyed by the entity moniker; thread the inspector layer FQM through to set `parentLayerFq`.
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-layer.tsx` — confirm the `LAYER_Z_TIERS` table (lines 107-116) handles a panel-tier descendant under `inspector` correctly (it already falls back to `parentTier + 20` for unnamed tiers).

-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.guards.node.test.ts` — the existing source-level guard "permits a single FocusScope import" (line 101 onwards) is now stale and must flip to forbidding `<FocusScope>` here and asserting the nested `<FocusLayer>` wrap.
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.test.tsx` and `inspector.close-restores-focus.browser.test.tsx` — assertions that pin the old single-layer-with-scope shape need updating.

-

-The original race (the real reason iteration 3 was reached for):
-- `<FocusLayer>`'s `registerLayerRegistry` runs in a `useEffect` at the layer level.
