+Re-reviewed by reviewer subagent after the implementer moved `<FocusLayer>` registration from `useEffect` to render-time (with a ref-guarded idempotency check) so descendant `useEffect`s see ancestor layers already pushed and registered. The fix is **correct and well-justified** — a render-time push is genuinely the only React mechanism that gives parent-before-child setup ordering (`useLayoutEffect` is also child-first; `useInsertionEffect` likewise). The new regression test is real, mounts the full app shell, and the strict-Rust-mirror invoke handler genuinely rejects undefined snapshots and missing-FQM scopes — the test would fail on `main` before the fix lands. Three soft findings; none are blockers.
+
+### Warnings
+
+- [x] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-layer.tsx` (file-header docstring lines 22-30) — the "Lifecycle" section still describes the layer's behaviour as "Mount: ... invokes `spatial_push_layer` ... Unmount: invokes `spatial_pop_layer`" without flagging the unusual render-time invocation pattern. The detailed inline comment block at the render-time site (the "Eager top-down registration" block) explains it well, but a reader skimming the file's header docstring will miss the design choice and may try to "fix" the render-time push back to a `useEffect`. Update the lifecycle bullet to read e.g. "Push is dispatched during render (top-down) so descendant `useEffect`s see ancestor layers already populated; pop runs on unmount via a `useEffect` cleanup. See the 'Eager top-down registration' block below for the rationale." Why: the doc comment is the contract; future maintainers should not have to reverse-engineer the unusual pattern from the implementation.
+
+- [x] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-layer.tsx` (the "Eager top-down registration" block, around the render-time `pushLayer` / `registerLayerRegistry` site) — render-time side effects are safe today only because the codebase contains no `<Suspense>`, `startTransition`, `useTransition`, or `useDeferredValue`. If a future change introduces any of those above a `<FocusLayer>` (or wraps a focus-state change in a transition), React may abandon a render after the side effects fire. Because the unmount cleanup lives in a `useEffect` that only attaches on commit, an abandoned render leaks a kernel-side `pushLayer` (and a `layerRegistriesRef` entry) that is never popped. Add a comment near the render-time block warning future maintainers, e.g. "WARNING: this pattern is safe only because the app does not use Suspense or transitions above any FocusLayer. Introducing either could cause render-abandonment to leak kernel-side layer pushes; migrate this back to a commit-time mechanism (or add a render-id tracking refcount) before adding such boundaries." Why: the invariant is real but invisible — a future PR adding `<Suspense>` for code-splitting could regress focus state in ways no one would attribute to this primitive.
+
+### Nits
+
+- [x] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-layer.tsx` (the unmount-cleanup `useEffect` with empty deps and the `// eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` suppression) — the empty-deps + lint suppression combination is itself a smell that future readers may mistake for a bug. The current comment justifies it, but consider strengthening the explanation: explicitly note that the cleanup reads from `eagerRef.current` (always live) and `popLayer` (closure-captured but functionally stable across renders for our purposes), and that re-running the effect on dep changes would race with the render-time tear-down + re-publish path. The current wording ("re-running on every change would race") is accurate but terse.
+
+### Things that look right
+
+- The render-time push pattern is the **right mechanism** for parent-before-child setup ordering. `useLayoutEffect` and `useInsertionEffect` are both child-first; only render itself is top-down in React. The implementer correctly identified that the `useEffect`-based design was structurally unable to satisfy the ordering invariant, and chose the only React mechanism that can.
+- StrictMode safety is verified: the `eagerRef.current === null || eagerRef.current.fq !== fq` guard correctly skips the second render in StrictMode's intentional double-invocation. The simulated unmount-remount cycle correctly tears down (via the `useEffect` cleanup, which sets `eagerRef.current = null`) and re-publishes on the second mount. The pre-existing test "layer key is stable across re-renders" in `focus-layer.test.tsx` continues to pin this invariant (one push per re-render).
+- FQM-swap path: when `name` or `parentLayerFq` changes mid-life, the prior `unregister()` and `popLayer(prior fq)` run synchronously during render before the new push fires. Consistent with the kernel's expectations.
+- Multi-window safety holds: each Tauri window has its own React tree, its own `SpatialFocusProvider` (and thus its own `layerRegistriesRef` map), and the kernel's `window_label_from(&window)` derivation in `kanban-app/src/commands.rs:2415` keys per-window state separately. The render-time push composes the FQM from the window-segment so cross-window collisions are structurally impossible.
+- The new regression test (`inspector.first-field-focus-race.browser.test.tsx`) is **rigorous**: it mounts the full app shell (`<AppShell><InspectorsContainer /></AppShell>` inside the real provider stack), installs a strict-Rust-mirror invoke handler that genuinely rejects `snapshot: undefined`, missing-from-`layers`, and missing-from-`scopes`, and asserts both the kernel's `currentFocus.fq` advances into `/window/inspector/task:T1/` AND the IPC ordering (`push:task:T1` arrives before `focus:.../field:task:T1.title`). The test would fail on `main` (where the IPC dispatches `spatial_focus` with `snapshot: undefined`).
+- All earlier findings are addressed:

-## Architecture (user-confirmed, HARD requirement)
+## Architecture (user-confirmed, simple)

-**There is ONE `<FocusLayer name="inspector">` for ALL open inspector panels.** Each panel is content inside that one shared layer — NOT its own layer. The inspector layer sits above the window layer. When the inspector layer is up, focus / jump / navigation must be confined to that single inspector layer.
+There are two layers we care about:

-The previous direction (one `<FocusLayer>` per panel) was wrong. Revert it.
+- **Window layer** — the base. Always present. Cards / columns / navbar are scopes inside it. `s` (jump-to), arrow nav, click-to-focus all work on the window layer. **This must keep working unchanged.**
+- **Inspector layer** — singular. ONE `<FocusLayer name="inspector">` for the whole window, mounted when any inspector panel is open. All open panels are content inside that one layer. Field rows are scopes inside that one layer. When the inspector layer is up, focus / jump / arrow-nav route to it (not to the window).

-The iteration-3 render-time push pattern (added to `<FocusLayer>` itself) was solving a fictional problem caused by the per-panel split and has introduced regressions on the window root layer too. Revert it.
+That's it. No per-panel layers. No render-time side effects in the FocusLayer primitive. No new architectural concepts.

-## Current production symptoms (confirmed by user)
+## Where we are now (broken state, after iters 1 and 3)

-1. **No inspector open: pressing `s` shows zero jump labels.** Jump-To enumerates scopes in the layer that owns prior focus. With nothing focused on the window, enumeration falls back, but no pills paint. Root regression — this was working before iteration 3.
+Iter 1 changed `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.tsx` to wrap each panel in its own nested `<FocusLayer>`. **Wrong** — there should be ONE inspector layer, not one per panel.

-2. **Inspector open: focus does land in the inspector field after iteration 3** (arrow-key nav inside the panel works). That part of iteration 3 succeeded.
+Iter 3 changed `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-layer.tsx` to push the layer at render-time (instead of in a `useEffect`). **Wrong** — render-time side effects were a code smell from the start, and they broke the window layer's normal operation: with no inspector open, pressing `s` shows zero jump labels (regression on the window-root layer that used to work).

-3. **Inspector open + press `s`: backdrop darkens the window, no pills appear.** Two stacked bugs:
-   - **Z-index**: `<JumpPill>` and the jump-to backdrop in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.tsx` have no `z-index`. The inspector panel uses `z-30` which paints above any z-auto sibling — so the backdrop only darkens the area outside the inspector panel and the pills paint at field coordinates but are hidden by the inspector panel above them. Confirmed by reading `jump-to-overlay.tsx:347-377` (chrome `<div data-testid="jump-to-overlay">` with no z-index, backdrop `fixed inset-0 ... bg-black/30`, pill `fixed left:rect.left+4 top:rect.top+4` — all z-auto).
-   - Even setting that aside, with NO inspector and pressing `s`, no pills paint, suggesting the iter-3 render-time push broke the window layer's registration timing too.

+- "when there is NO inspector, I hit s and get no jump labels"

Field `<FocusScope>`s register directly with the single inspector layer's `LayerScopeRegistry`.

+### 2. Revert iter 1

+Restore `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.tsx` to the single-inspector-layer shape: ONE outer `<FocusLayer name="inspector">`, panels rendered as plain content inside (no nested layer per panel, no `<FocusScope>` wrap around panel bodies — fields register directly under the inspector layer). Update or delete `inspectors-container.layer-containment.browser.test.tsx`, `inspector.cross-panel-nav.browser.test.tsx`, `inspector.layer-shape.browser.test.tsx`, `inspector.entity-zone-barrier.browser.test.tsx`, `inspector.kernel-focus-advance.browser.test.tsx`, and `inspectors-container.guards.node.test.ts` to match.

-The original race (the real reason iteration 3 was reached for):

-- inspector panel: `z-30`
-- inspector backdrop: `z-20`
-- palette / dialogs / etc.: see `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-layer.tsx::LAYER_Z_TIERS` (palette: 70).

Add a small SpatialFocusActions API like `topLayerFq()` (or expose the kernel's layer stack), and have `useJumpTargets` use it. With no inspector open, `topLayerFq()` returns `/window`. +title: Make focus / jump / nav target the topmost layer (modal-layer model)

-## Architecture (user-confirmed, simple)
+## The model (user's words)

-There are two layers we care about:
+> "on a layer -- i want to be able to click or jump to focus, then navigate. when i inspect, a new layer for all inspectors is above the window - i want that to be focus on initial load, and click and jump is now focus and jump - ON THE TOP LAYER -- lower layers will only respond to a click to just dismiss higher layers -- much like `ESCAPE` when not captured by an editor needs to run the dismiss command."

-- **Window layer** — the base. Always present. Cards / columns / navbar are scopes inside it. `s` (jump-to), arrow nav, click-to-focus all work on the window layer. **This must keep working unchanged.**
-- **Inspector layer** — singular. ONE `<FocusLayer name="inspector">` for the whole window, mounted when any inspector panel is open. All open panels are content inside that one layer. Field rows are scopes inside that one layer. When the inspector layer is up, focus / jump / arrow-nav route to it (not to the window).
+There is a layer stack. The **topmost** (deepest pushed) layer is the **active layer**. The model:

-That's it. No per-panel layers. No render-time side effects in the FocusLayer primitive. No new architectural concepts.
+| Event | Behavior |
+|-------|----------|
+| Click inside active layer's content | Focus that scope |
+| Click outside active layer's content (e.g. backdrop / lower-layer area) | Dismiss the active layer (do NOT focus the clicked element) |
+| Press `s` (jump) | Pills enumerate the active layer's scopes |
+| Arrow keys (when not in an editor) | Navigate within the active layer |
+| `Escape` (when not in an editor) | Dismiss the active layer |
+| New layer mounts | Focus auto-claims to its first interior scope |
+| Layer pops | Focus restores to the parent layer's `last_focused` |

-## Where we are now (broken state, after iters 1 and 3)
+When no inspector is open, the **window** layer is active. When an inspector is open, the **inspector** layer (singular, shared by all panels) is active.

+## Architecture (user-confirmed, simple)

-> "on a layer -- i want to be able to click or jump to focus, then navigate. when i inspect, a new layer for all inspectors is above the window - i want that to be focus on initial load, and click and jump is now focus and jump - ON THE TOP LAYER -- lower layers will only respond to a click to just dismiss higher layers -- much like `ESCAPE` when not captured by an editor needs to run the dismiss command."
+There are two layers we care about:

-There is a layer stack. The **topmost** (deepest pushed) layer is the **active layer**. The model:
+- **Window layer** — the base. Always present. Cards / columns / navbar are scopes inside it. `s` (jump-to), arrow nav, click-to-focus all work on the window layer. **This must keep working unchanged.**
+- **Inspector layer** — singular. ONE `<FocusLayer name="inspector">` for the whole window, mounted when any inspector panel is open. All open panels are content inside that one layer. Field rows are scopes inside that one layer. When the inspector layer is up, focus / jump / arrow-nav route to it (not to the window).

-| Event | Behavior |
-|-------|----------|
-| Click inside active layer's content | Focus that scope |
-| Click outside active layer's content (e.g. backdrop / lower-layer area) | Dismiss the active layer (do NOT focus the clicked element) |
-| Press `s` (jump) | Pills enumerate the active layer's scopes |
-| Arrow keys (when not in an editor) | Navigate within the active layer |
-| `Escape` (when not in an editor) | Dismiss the active layer |
-| New layer mounts | Focus auto-claims to its first interior scope |
-| Layer pops | Focus restores to the parent layer's `last_focused` |
+That's it. No per-panel layers. No render-time side effects in the FocusLayer primitive. No new architectural concepts.

 > "on a layer -- i want to be able to click or jump to focus, then navigate. when i inspect, a new layer for all inspectors is above the window - i want that to be focus on initial load, and click and jump is now focus and jump - ON THE TOP LAYER -- lower layers will only respond to a click to just dismiss higher layers -- much like `ESCAPE` when not captured by an editor needs to run the dismiss command."

+> "i want you to make sure focus and dismiss are proper commands in our command system, not just hard coded."
+
 There is a layer stack. The **topmost** (deepest pushed) layer is the **active layer**. The model:

-| Event | Behavior |
-|-------|----------|
-| Click inside active layer's content | Focus that scope |
-| Click outside active layer's content (e.g. backdrop / lower-layer area) | Dismiss the active layer (do NOT focus the clicked element) |
-| Press `s` (jump) | Pills enumerate the active layer's scopes |
-| Arrow keys (when not in an editor) | Navigate within the active layer |
-| `Escape` (when not in an editor) | Dismiss the active layer |
-| New layer mounts | Focus auto-claims to its first interior scope |
-| Layer pops | Focus restores to the parent layer's `last_focused` |
+| Event | Behavior | Wire via |
+|-------|----------|----------|
+| Click inside active layer's content | Focus that scope | `nav.focus` command |
+| Click outside active layer's content (backdrop / lower-layer area) | Dismiss the active layer | `app.dismiss` command |
+| Press `s` (jump) | Pills enumerate the active layer's scopes | (existing `nav.jump`) |
+| Jump-to match | Focus the matched scope | `nav.focus` command |
+| Arrow keys (not in editor) | Navigate within the active layer | (existing `nav.up/down/left/right`) |
+| `Escape` (not in editor) | Dismiss the active layer | `app.dismiss` command |
+| New layer mounts | Auto-focus its first interior scope | `nav.focus` command |
+| Layer pops | Focus restores to parent's `last_focused` | (existing kernel mechanism) |

 When no inspector is open, the **window** layer is active. When an inspector is open, the **inspector** layer (singular, shared by all panels) is active.

+| Click inside active layer's content | Focus that scope |
+| Click outside active layer's content (e.g. backdrop / lower-layer area) | Dismiss the active layer (do NOT focus the clicked element) |
+| Press `s` (jump) | Pills enumerate the active layer's scopes |
+| Arrow keys (when not in an editor) | Navigate within the active layer |
+| `Escape` (when not in an editor) | Dismiss the active layer |
+| New layer mounts | Focus auto-claims to its first interior scope |
+| Layer pops | Focus restores to the parent layer's `last_focused` |

 When no inspector is open, the **window** layer is active. When an inspector is open, the **inspector** layer (singular, shared by all panels) is active.

 There is a layer stack. The **topmost** (deepest pushed) layer is the **active layer**. The model:

-| Event | Behavior |
-|-------|----------|
-| Click inside active layer's content | Focus that scope |
-| Click outside active layer's content (e.g. backdrop / lower-layer area) | Dismiss the active layer (do NOT focus the clicked element) |
-| Press `s` (jump) | Pills enumerate the active layer's scopes |
-| Arrow keys (when not in an editor) | Navigate within the active layer |
-| `Escape` (when not in an editor) | Dismiss the active layer |
-| New layer mounts | Focus auto-claims to its first interior scope |
-| Layer pops | Focus restores to the parent layer's `last_focused` |

+| Press `s` (jump) | Pills enumerate the active layer's scopes | (existing `nav.jump`) |
+| Jump-to match | Focus the matched scope | `nav.focus` command |
+| Arrow keys (not in editor) | Navigate within the active layer | (existing `nav.up/down/left/right`) |
+| `Escape` (not in editor) | Dismiss the active layer | `app.dismiss` command |
+| New layer mounts | Auto-focus its first interior scope | `nav.focus` command |
+| Layer pops | Focus restores to parent's `last_focused` | (existing kernel mechanism) |

 When no inspector is open, the **window** layer is active. When an inspector is open, the **inspector** layer (singular, shared by all panels) is active.

 There is a layer stack. The **topmost** (deepest pushed) layer is the **active layer**. The model:

-| Event | Behavior | Wire via |
-|-------|----------|----------|
-| Click inside active layer's content | Focus that scope | `nav.focus` command |
-| Click outside active layer's content (backdrop / lower-layer area) | Dismiss the active layer | `app.dismiss` command |
-| Press `s` (jump) | Pills enumerate the active layer's scopes | (existing `nav.jump`) |
-| Jump-to match | Focus the matched scope | `nav.focus` command |
-| Arrow keys (not in editor) | Navigate within the active layer | (existing `nav.up/down/left/right`) |
-| `Escape` (not in editor) | Dismiss the active layer | `app.dismiss` command |
-| New layer mounts | Auto-focus its first interior scope | `nav.focus` command |
-| Layer pops | Focus restores to parent's `last_focused` | (existing kernel mechanism) |
+| Event | Behavior |
+|-------|----------|
+| Click inside active layer's content | Focus that scope |
+| Click outside active layer's content (e.g. backdrop / lower-layer area) | Dismiss the active layer (do NOT focus the clicked element) |
+| Press `s` (jump) | Pills enumerate the active layer's scopes |
+| Arrow keys (when not in an editor) | Navigate within the active layer |
+| `Escape` (when not in an editor) | Dismiss the active layer |
+| New layer mounts | Focus auto-claims to its first interior scope |
+| Layer pops | Focus restores to the parent layer's `last_focused` |

 When no inspector is open, the **window** layer is active. When an inspector is open, the **inspector** layer (singular, shared by all panels) is active.

-- [ ] `app.dismiss` is dispatched (not hard-coded `closeAll` / `setJumpOpen(false)` / etc.) for: inspector backdrop click, jump-to backdrop click, jump-to no-match, escape (already wired). Direct close-X calls stay where they have a specific target.
+- [ ] `nav.focus` command exists in the global command registry. Args: `{ fq }`. Execute calls the existing kernel-facing `setFocus`. It is the ONE place in the UI that calls `setFocus(fq)` for non-null `fq`.
+- [ ] Every callsite that previously called `setFocus(fq)` directly now dispatches `nav.focus`.
+- [ ] `nav.jump` (existing) targets the topmost layer via `useJumpTargets` reading `topLayerFq()`.
+- [ ] `app.dismiss` (existing) is smart about the topmost layer. With only the window layer mounted, it is a no-op. With inspector / palette / jump-to on top, it closes that layer.
+- [ ] Backdrop and "close active layer" code in the frontend dispatches `app.dismiss` instead of hard-coding which layer to close.

 - [ ] `<FocusLayer>` primitive runs push/pop in `useEffect`. NO render-time side effects.
-- [ ] `SpatialFocusActions.topLayerFq()` exists and returns the topmost layer FQM (or null when no layer is mounted).
-- [ ] `useJumpTargets` uses `topLayerFq()` to pick the enumeration target.
-- [ ] No inspector open: pressing `s` paints pills on cards / columns / navbar (window-layer scopes). Click cards focuses them. Arrow keys navigate the board. **Window layer keeps working as before.**
-- [ ] Inspector open: focus auto-claims to the first field on mount via `nav.focus`. Pressing `s` paints pills on inspector field scopes (NOT on board cards). Pills render above the inspector panel (z > 30). Arrow keys navigate fields. Click on the inspector backdrop dispatches `app.dismiss` and dismisses the inspector.

-There is a layer stack. The **topmost** (deepest pushed) layer is the **active layer**. The window layer is the bottom-most layer and cannot be dismissed.
+There is a layer stack. The **topmost** (deepest pushed) layer is the **active layer**. The model:

 | Event | Behavior | Wire via |
 |-------|----------|----------|
-| Click inside active layer's content | Focus that scope | `nav.focus` (NEW command) |
-| Click outside active layer's content (backdrop / lower-layer area) | Dismiss the active layer | `app.dismiss` (existing) |
-| Press `s` (jump) | Pills enumerate the active layer's scopes | `nav.jump` (existing) — make top-layer-aware |
-| Jump-to match | Focus the matched scope | `nav.focus` (NEW command) |
-| Arrow keys (not in editor) | Navigate within the active layer | (existing `nav.up/down/left/right` — already layer-scoped via snapshot) |
-| `Escape` (not in editor) | Dismiss the active layer | `app.dismiss` (existing) |
-| New layer mounts | Auto-focus its first interior scope | `nav.focus` (NEW command) |
-| `app.dismiss` while window is the only layer | No-op | Existing handler returns early |
+| Click inside active layer's content | Focus that scope | `nav.focus` command |
+| Click outside active layer's content (backdrop / lower-layer area) | Dismiss the active layer | `app.dismiss` command |
+| Press `s` (jump) | Pills enumerate the active layer's scopes | (existing `nav.jump`) |
+| Jump-to match | Focus the matched scope | `nav.focus` command |
+| Arrow keys (not in editor) | Navigate within the active layer | (existing `nav.up/down/left/right`) |
+| `Escape` (not in editor) | Dismiss the active layer | `app.dismiss` command |
+| New layer mounts | Auto-focus its first interior scope | `nav.focus` command |
+| Layer pops | Focus restores to parent's `last_focused` | (existing kernel mechanism) |
+
+When no inspector is open, the **window** layer is active. When an inspector is open, the **inspector** layer (singular, shared by all panels) is active.

 ## Acceptance Criteria

-- [ ] `nav.focus` command exists in the global command registry. Args: `{ fq }`. Execute calls the existing kernel-facing `setFocus`. It is the ONE place in the UI that calls `setFocus(fq)` for non-null `fq`.
-- [ ] Every callsite that previously called `setFocus(fq)` directly now dispatches `nav.focus`.
-- [ ] `nav.jump` (existing) targets the topmost layer via `useJumpTargets` reading `topLayerFq()`.
-- [ ] `app.dismiss` (existing) is smart about the topmost layer. With only the window layer mounted, it is a no-op. With inspector / palette / jump-to on top, it closes that layer.
-- [ ] Backdrop and "close active layer" code in the frontend dispatches `app.dismiss` instead of hard-coding which layer to close.
+- [ ] `nav.focus` command exists in the global command registry. Args: `{ fq: FullyQualifiedMoniker }`. Execute calls the existing kernel-facing `setFocus`.
+- [ ] Every callsite that previously called `setFocus(fq)` directly (focus-scope click handler, jump-to match, inspector auto-focus, etc.) now dispatches `nav.focus` instead. The kernel-facing `setFocus` is called from exactly one place: the `nav.focus` command's execute closure.
+- [ ] `app.dismiss` is dispatched (not hard-coded `closeAll` / `setJumpOpen(false)` / etc.) for: inspector backdrop click, jump-to backdrop click, jump-to no-match, escape (already wired). Direct close-X calls stay where they have a specific target.
 - [ ] ONE shared `<FocusLayer name="inspector">` in `inspectors-container.tsx`. No per-panel layers. No `<FocusScope>` wrap per panel.
 - [ ] `<FocusLayer>` primitive runs push/pop in `useEffect`. NO render-time side effects.
-- [ ] `SpatialFocusActions.topLayerFq()` exists.
-- [ ] No inspector open: pressing `s` paints pills on cards / columns / navbar. Click cards focuses them. Arrow keys navigate the board. Window layer keeps working as before.
-- [ ] Inspector open: focus auto-claims to first field on mount via `nav.focus`. Pressing `s` paints pills on inspector field scopes. Pills render above inspector panel (z > 30). Arrow keys navigate fields. Click on inspector backdrop dispatches `app.dismiss` and dismisses the inspector.
+- [ ] `SpatialFocusActions.topLayerFq()` exists and returns the topmost layer FQM (or null when no layer is mounted).
+- [ ] `useJumpTargets` uses `topLayerFq()` to pick the enumeration target.
+- [ ] No inspector open: pressing `s` paints pills on cards / columns / navbar (window-layer scopes). Click cards focuses them. Arrow keys navigate the board. **Window layer keeps working as before.**
+- [ ] Inspector open: focus auto-claims to the first field on mount via `nav.focus`. Pressing `s` paints pills on inspector field scopes (NOT on board cards). Pills render above the inspector panel (z > 30). Arrow keys navigate fields. Click on the inspector backdrop dispatches `app.dismiss` and dismisses the inspector.
 - [ ] Full UI suite, Rust workspace, tsc, clippy all green/clean.

-- Do not invent new commands. `nav.focus` is the only new one. `nav.jump` and `app.dismiss` exist — make them smarter, don't replace.

+There is a layer stack. The **topmost** (deepest pushed) layer is the **active layer**. The model:

 | Event | Behavior | Wire via |
 |-------|----------|----------|
-| Click inside active layer's content | Focus that scope | `nav.focus` (NEW command) |
-| Click outside active layer's content (backdrop / lower-layer area) | Dismiss the active layer | `app.dismiss` (existing) |
-| Press `s` (jump) | Pills enumerate the active layer's scopes | `nav.jump` (existing) — make top-layer-aware |

-| `Escape` (not in editor) | Dismiss the active layer | `app.dismiss` (existing) |
-| New layer mounts | Auto-focus its first interior scope | `nav.focus` (NEW command) |
-| `app.dismiss` while window is the only layer | No-op | Existing handler returns early |
+| Click inside active layer's content | Focus that scope | `nav.focus` command |
+| Click outside active layer's content (backdrop / lower-layer area) | Dismiss the active layer | `app.dismiss` command |
+| Press `s` (jump) | Pills enumerate the active layer's scopes | (existing `nav.jump`) |
+| Jump-to match | Focus the matched scope | `nav.focus` command |
+| Arrow keys (not in editor) | Navigate within the active layer | (existing `nav.up/down/left/right`) |
+| `Escape` (not in editor) | Dismiss the active layer | `app.dismiss` command |
+| New layer mounts | Auto-focus its first interior scope | `nav.focus` command |
+| Layer pops | Focus restores to parent's `last_focused` | (existing kernel mechanism) |
+
+When no inspector is open, the **window** layer is active. When an inspector is open, the **inspector** layer (singular, shared by all panels) is active.

+
+After the above changes, **69 of 2081 UI tests fail** — all with the same shape: a test mounts `<FocusScope>` directly inside a `<CommandScopeProvider commands={[]}>` (no `<AppShell>`) and asserts that a click dispatches `spatial_focus` IPC. +## Review Findings (2026-05-10 17:18)

+### Nits
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/nav-focus.source-guard.node.test.ts` — the `arg.includes(":")` heuristic on the scanned-line filter accepts any `setFocus(<arg>)` call whose arg contains a colon, including string-literal FQ calls like `setFocus("entity:foo")`. The intent is to skip TypeScript signature lines like `setFocus: (fq: ...) => void`, but a future regression that re-introduced a literal-FQ direct call would slip past. Tightening the heuristic — for example, requiring the line to also contain `: (` or `=>` to qualify as a signature, OR explicitly disallowing arg patterns that start and end with a quote — would close this blind spot. Not load-bearing today; future-proofing only.
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/nav-focus.source-guard.node.test.ts:43-48` — the allowlist entries for `kanban-app/ui/src/components/app-shell.tsx` and `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-inspector.tsx` are defensive: those files do not currently have any line that matches the regex `(\w+\.)?setFocus\(`. Both call sites use `setFocusRef.current(...)` (a non-matching shape) for the legitimate non-null primitive calls. The allowlist comments describe these as live exceptions, but they are actually future-proofing slots. Either tighten the comments to say "future-proofing for a direct-call shape — not currently matched" or scrub the allowlist entries until a real direct call appears. Doc/intent clarity nit only.
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.tsx:72` — doc comment refers to `parentLayerKey` (the legacy variable name) while the actual prop on `<FocusLayer>` is `parentLayerFq`. Rename the reference in the comment to keep the prose consistent with the code.
